message:
type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding filters param to prompt decorator, from_str/from_file, and create_jinja_env helper

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding register_filter/unregister_filter global filter registry to prompts.py

type: QUESTION
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Confirming design to avoid clobbering: I am adding a filters= param to prompt()/Prompt.from_str()/Prompt.from_file() plus a create_jinja_env() helper that centralizes env creation+built-in filter registration. Will you be adding register_filter/unregister_filter as module-level functions that mutate a global dict, and expect create_jinja_env to apply globally-registered filters too? Want to make sure our two changes compose in the same env-creation helper rather than conflicting.

type: QUESTION
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: I'm adding register_filter/unregister_filter global registry that must merge into env.filters, local overriding global
blocked_on: need shape of your create_jinja_env helper signature so I can inject global filters without clobbering your local filters param

type: ANSWER
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Yes - confirmed: register_filter(name,func)/unregister_filter(name) mutate module-level _FILTERS dict; please have create_jinja_env(filters=None) apply built-ins first, then merge in _FILTERS (global registry), then merge in the local filters= param last so local overrides global overrides built-ins. I'll add register_filter/unregister_filter plus the merge-in of _FILTERS inside create_jinja_env once you land it - ping me when create_jinja_env is ready so I don't clobber your edit.

type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) is landed in outlines/prompts.py: builds env with loader arg, registers built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does 'if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters)' as the last step before return. Insert your global _FILTERS merge between the built-ins block and that final local-filters update line so ordering is builtins -> global -> local. Prompt.from_str/from_file and the prompt() decorator all call create_jinja_env already and pass their local filters dict through, so your registry merge inside create_jinja_env will automatically apply everywhere. Go ahead and add register_filter/unregister_filter + the merge; I'm done editing this file for my feature.

type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Landed register_filter/unregister_filter global registry with env.filters.update(_FILTERS) applied in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file, tests pass; patch.txt written
